items.name: ForgeConfig struct hierarchy
items.description: Define all configuration structs (ForgeConfig, SpecifyingConfig, PlanningConfig, ImplementingConfig, EvalConfig, AgentConfig, CrossRefConfig, ReconciliationConfig, DomainConfig, PathsConfig, LogsConfig, GeneralConfig). These replace the flat BatchSize/MinRounds/MaxRounds/UserGuided fields on ForgeState.
steps: Add AgentConfig struct with model, type, count fields
Add EvalConfig struct with MinRounds, MaxRounds, AgentConfig (embedded), EnableEvalOutput
Add CrossRefConfig, ReconciliationConfig structs
Add SpecifyingConfig, PlanningConfig (with SelfReview, PlanAllBeforeImplementing), ImplementingConfig
Add DomainConfig, PathsConfig, LogsConfig, GeneralConfig structs
Add ForgeConfig top-level struct combining all sub-configs

tests.description: ForgeConfig marshals/unmarshals to JSON matching state-persistence.md schema (model/type/count sub-objects, all fields present with correct types)
Default config values are correct (enable_commits=false, commit_strategy defaults, min/max round defaults)

items.name: ForgeState and sub-struct updates
items.description: Refactor ForgeState to hold ForgeConfig, add SessionID, add GeneratePlanningQueueState field, add CompletedPlan struct, update PlanningState.Completed type, add ImplementingState fields for multi-plan support. Add new phase and state constants.
steps: Add PhaseGeneratePlanningQueue constant = 'generate_planning_queue'
Add StateSelfReview constant = 'SELF_REVIEW'
Add GeneratePlanningQueueState struct with PlanQueueFile string field
Add CompletedPlan struct (ID, Name, Domain, File)
Remove BatchSize, MinRounds, MaxRounds, UserGuided from ForgeState
Add Config ForgeConfig, SessionID string, GeneratePlanningQueue *GeneratePlanningQueueState to ForgeState
Change PlanningState.Completed from []interface{} to []CompletedPlan
Add PlanQueue []PlanQueueEntry, CurrentPlanFile string, CurrentPlanDomain string to ImplementingState
Add DomainRoots map[string][]string to SpecifyingState (for set-roots data used by genqueue)
Add SpecCommits []string to PlanQueueEntry

tests.description: ForgeState with Config field round-trips through JSON marshal/unmarshal without data loss
ForgeState.GeneratePlanningQueue is omitempty — absent when nil in JSON output
PlanningState.Completed serializes as []CompletedPlan with id/name/domain/file fields

items.name: PlanItem Specs and Refs array fields
items.description: Change PlanItem.Spec (string) to Specs ([]string) and PlanItem.Ref (string) to Refs ([]string). The Specs field holds spec references for display; Refs holds notes file paths validated on disk.
steps: Change Spec string to Specs []string in PlanItem struct with json tag 'specs,omitempty'
Change Ref string to Refs []string in PlanItem struct with json tag 'refs,omitempty'

tests.description: PlanItem with multiple specs marshals to array in JSON
PlanItem with multiple refs marshals to array in JSON

items.name: TOML config parser and project root discovery
items.description: Implement .forgectl/config TOML parsing into ForgeConfig, project root discovery by walking up the directory tree, session ID generation, and default config merging.
steps: Add TOML dependency to go.mod (github.com/BurntSushi/toml or equivalent)
Create forgectl/state/config.go
Implement FindProjectRoot(startDir string) (string, error) — walks up until .forgectl/ found
Implement LoadConfig(projectRoot string) (ForgeConfig, error) — reads .forgectl/config TOML, applies defaults for missing fields
Implement applyDefaults(cfg *ForgeConfig) — fills in all zero values with spec-defined defaults
Implement GenerateSessionID() string — UUID v4 using crypto/rand
Implement ValidateConfig(cfg ForgeConfig) []string — returns constraint violations

tests.description: FindProjectRoot walks up from a nested subdirectory and finds .forgectl/
FindProjectRoot returns error when no .forgectl/ found in any parent
LoadConfig returns all defaults when .forgectl/config is absent
LoadConfig merges partial TOML (missing fields get defaults)
GenerateSessionID returns a valid UUID v4 format string
ValidateConfig returns error when commit_strategy is not one of the 5 valid values
ValidateConfig returns error when domain paths are nested (one is prefix of another)
ValidateConfig returns error when min_rounds > max_rounds for any eval config

items.name: Init command overhaul for TOML-driven config
items.description: Rewrite init command to read ForgeConfig from .forgectl/config TOML, remove --batch-size/--min-rounds/--max-rounds CLI flags, use project root discovery, generate session_id, validate config, and reject --phase generate_planning_queue.
steps: Remove --batch-size, --min-rounds, --max-rounds flags from init command
Call FindProjectRoot from init to discover .forgectl/
Call LoadConfig(projectRoot) to get ForgeConfig
Call ValidateConfig — print errors and exit 1 on failure
Validate --phase is one of specifying, planning, implementing (reject generate_planning_queue with specific error)
Validate domain config against spec queue when --phase specifying and domains are configured
Generate SessionID using GenerateSessionID()
Set ForgeState.Config = config, ForgeState.SessionID = sessionID
Update NewSpecifyingState, NewPlanningState, NewImplementingState to not take batch/rounds (read from Config)
Update status output header line 'Config: batch=N, rounds=M-N, guided=bool' to use Config fields

tests.description: init without .forgectl/config succeeds using all defaults
init reads batch from .forgectl/config TOML and stores in ForgeState.Config
init with --phase generate_planning_queue prints specific error and exits 1
init with invalid commit_strategy in .forgectl/config prints constraint violation and exits 1
init with nested domain paths in .forgectl/config prints error and exits 1
init generates a non-empty SessionID stored in state
init when .forgectl/ directory not found prints 'No .forgectl directory found.' and exits 1

items.name: ValidatePlanJSON updates for plural Specs/Refs and path resolution
items.description: Update ValidatePlanJSON to handle PlanItem.Specs ([]string, no disk check) and PlanItem.Refs ([]string, each validated relative to plan.json dir). Remove old Spec/Ref string checks.
steps: In ValidatePlanJSON: remove check for items[].Spec (old string field)
In ValidatePlanJSON: remove check for items[].Ref file existence
Add loop: for each item, for each path in item.Refs, resolve relative to baseDir and check os.Stat — add error if missing
items[].Specs: no file existence check (display references only, #anchors permitted)
Update test helpers in validate_test.go to use Specs/Refs arrays instead of Spec/Ref strings

tests.description: ValidatePlanJSON passes for item with Refs: ['notes/foo.md'] when file exists relative to baseDir
ValidatePlanJSON fails for item with Refs: ['notes/missing.md'] when file does not exist
ValidatePlanJSON passes for item with Specs: ['foo.md#section'] — anchor in spec ref is permitted
ValidatePlanJSON passes for item with Specs: ['spec1.md', 'spec2.md'] — multiple spec refs allowed

items.name: enable_eval_output, enable_commits flag gating, and --guided handling
items.description: Gate --eval-report on Config.*.Eval.EnableEvalOutput across all evaluation states (EVALUATE in specifying/planning/implementing, RECONCILE_EVAL, CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL). Gate --message on Config.General.EnableCommits at COMPLETE, planning ACCEPT, implementing IMPLEMENT (first round), and implementing COMMIT. Maintain --guided/--no-guided updating Config.General.UserGuided on every advance call including at PHASE_SHIFT. Warn when optional flags are provided but disabled.
steps: In validateAdvanceFlags: when state is an eval state and EnableEvalOutput=false, do not require --eval-report; if provided, print warning 'ignoring --eval-report: eval output is not enabled'
In validateAdvanceFlags: when state is an eval state and EnableEvalOutput=true, require --eval-report as before
In advanceSpecifying COMPLETE: if EnableCommits=true and message='', return error requiring --message; if EnableCommits=false and message!='', print warning and ignore
In advancePlanning ACCEPT: same enable_commits gating
In advanceImplementing IMPLEMENT (first round, EvalRound==0): if EnableCommits=true and message='', return error; if EnableCommits=false and message!='', print warning and ignore
In advanceImplementing COMMIT: if EnableCommits=true and message='', return error; if EnableCommits=false and message!='', print warning
In EvalRecord storage: when EnableEvalOutput=false, EvalReport field should be empty string (not stored)
In Advance() top-level: before dispatching to phase handler, if in.Guided != nil, update s.Config.General.UserGuided — this applies at all states including PHASE_SHIFT

tests.description: advance in EVALUATE with EnableEvalOutput=false succeeds without --eval-report
advance in EVALUATE with EnableEvalOutput=true fails without --eval-report
advance in EVALUATE with EnableEvalOutput=false and --eval-report provided prints warning and proceeds
advance at COMPLETE with EnableCommits=true and no --message fails
advance at COMPLETE with EnableCommits=false and --message provided prints warning and proceeds
advance at implementing IMPLEMENT (first round, EvalRound==0) with EnableCommits=true and no --message fails
advance at implementing IMPLEMENT with EnableCommits=false and --message provided prints warning and proceeds
advance with --guided=true at any state (including PHASE_SHIFT) sets Config.General.UserGuided=true in persisted state
advance with --no-guided at PHASE_SHIFT sets Config.General.UserGuided=false before the transition proceeds

items.name: SELF_REVIEW state in planning phase
items.description: Add SELF_REVIEW state between VALIDATE (success) and EVALUATE in the planning phase state machine, controlled by Config.Planning.SelfReview. Advance from SELF_REVIEW transitions to EVALUATE.
steps: In advancePlanningFromDraftOrRefine: after successful validation, check Config.Planning.SelfReview; if true, set state=SELF_REVIEW and return; if false, set state=EVALUATE as before
In advancePlanning: add SELF_REVIEW case — set state=EVALUATE and return
In PrintAdvanceOutput: add SELF_REVIEW output case (see output.md for format)

tests.description: Planning with SelfReview=true transitions VALIDATE→SELF_REVIEW→EVALUATE
Planning with SelfReview=false transitions VALIDATE→EVALUATE (skips SELF_REVIEW)

items.name: generate_planning_queue phase state machine
items.description: Implement the full generate_planning_queue phase: ORIENT auto-generates plan-queue.json from completed specs, REFINE validates on advance, PHASE_SHIFT transitions to planning. Update specifying COMPLETE to transition to generate_planning_queue PHASE_SHIFT instead of planning PHASE_SHIFT.
steps: In Advance(): add case PhaseGeneratePlanningQueue → advanceGeneratePlanningQueue(s, in, dir)
Implement advanceGeneratePlanningQueue: handle ORIENT, REFINE, PHASE_SHIFT states
ORIENT advance: set state=REFINE, print REFINE output
REFINE advance: read plan-queue.json, ValidatePlanQueue; if invalid print errors stay REFINE; if valid set state=PHASE_SHIFT with PhaseShift{From:genqueue, To:planning}
PHASE_SHIFT advance (in advancePhaseShift): when From=generate_planning_queue — without --from: read plan-queue from state file, populate planning; with --from: validate override then populate
Implement autoGeneratePlanQueue(s, projectRoot) — group completed specs by domain, build PlanQueueEntry per domain, write to state_dir/plan-queue.json
In advancePhaseShift: when From=specifying, To=generate_planning_queue — call autoGeneratePlanQueue, set GeneratePlanningQueueState, set phase=generate_planning_queue, state=ORIENT
In advanceSpecifying COMPLETE: change PhaseShift destination from 'planning' to 'generate_planning_queue'

tests.description: specifying COMPLETE → PHASE_SHIFT with From=specifying, To=generate_planning_queue
PHASE_SHIFT(specifying) advance without --from: transitions to generate_planning_queue ORIENT and writes plan-queue.json
Auto-generated plan-queue.json groups specs by domain and sets code_search_roots default to ['<domain>/']
Auto-generated plan-queue.json uses set-roots data when available for a domain
generate_planning_queue ORIENT → REFINE on advance
generate_planning_queue REFINE advance: invalid plan-queue.json prints errors and stays REFINE
generate_planning_queue REFINE advance: valid plan-queue.json transitions to PHASE_SHIFT(genqueue→planning)
generate_planning_queue PHASE_SHIFT advance: transitions to planning ORIENT with correct CurrentPlan
PHASE_SHIFT(specifying) advance with --from: skips generate_planning_queue, transitions directly to planning ORIENT
PHASE_SHIFT(specifying) advance with invalid --from: prints errors, stays PHASE_SHIFT

items.name: Multi-plan phase transitions (plan_all_before_implementing)
items.description: Implement plan_all_before_implementing-aware phase transitions: planning ACCEPT fires differently depending on mode, implementing DONE checks for remaining plans. Handle all four cross-phase and same-phase PHASE_SHIFT variants. Enforce planning DONE flag rejection.
steps: In advancePlanning ACCEPT: read Config.Planning.PlanAllBeforeImplementing
If PlanAllBeforeImplementing=false: always → PHASE_SHIFT(planning→implementing) as before
If PlanAllBeforeImplementing=true AND Planning.Queue non-empty: move CurrentPlan to Planning.Completed, pull next plan, set PHASE_SHIFT(planning→planning)
If PlanAllBeforeImplementing=true AND Planning.Queue empty: move CurrentPlan to Planning.Completed, set PHASE_SHIFT(planning→implementing), populate Implementing.PlanQueue from Planning.Completed
In advancePlanning DONE: reject all flags — return error 'DONE is a pass-through state. No flags accepted.' if any flag (verdict, eval-report, message, from, file) is provided
In advancePhaseShift for planning→planning: reset Planning round/evals, set state=ORIENT, print ORIENT for new CurrentPlan
In advancePhaseShift for planning→implementing: validate+mutate plan.json, set phase=implementing, state=ORIENT, set CurrentPlanFile/CurrentPlanDomain on ImplementingState
In advanceImplementing DONE: check Implementing.PlanQueue (all-first) or Planning.Queue (interleaved)
If plans remain and PlanAllBeforeImplementing=false: set PHASE_SHIFT(implementing→planning)
If plans remain and PlanAllBeforeImplementing=true: set PHASE_SHIFT(implementing→implementing), pop next plan from Implementing.PlanQueue
In advancePhaseShift for implementing→planning: set phase=planning, pop from Planning.Queue, reset round/evals, state=ORIENT
In advancePhaseShift for implementing→implementing: read+validate+mutate next plan.json, set state=ORIENT
When no plans remain: enter terminal DONE (session complete)

tests.description: PlanAllBeforeImplementing=false: planning ACCEPT → PHASE_SHIFT(planning→implementing) regardless of queue
PlanAllBeforeImplementing=true with plans in queue: planning ACCEPT → PHASE_SHIFT(planning→planning)
PlanAllBeforeImplementing=true with empty queue: planning ACCEPT → PHASE_SHIFT(planning→implementing), implementing PlanQueue populated
PHASE_SHIFT(planning→planning) advance: new CurrentPlan set, state=ORIENT
Implementing DONE with Planning.Queue non-empty and PlanAllBeforeImplementing=false: → PHASE_SHIFT(implementing→planning)
PHASE_SHIFT(implementing→planning) advance: phase=planning, new plan from queue, state=ORIENT
Implementing DONE with Implementing.PlanQueue non-empty and PlanAllBeforeImplementing=true: → PHASE_SHIFT(implementing→implementing)
PHASE_SHIFT(implementing→implementing) advance: reads, validates, and mutates next plan.json; state=ORIENT
Implementing DONE with no plans remaining: terminal DONE state with session summary
Single domain: implementing DONE with empty Planning.Queue goes to terminal DONE (not phase shift)
advance at planning DONE with --verdict flag returns 'DONE is a pass-through state. No flags accepted.' and exits 1
advance with --guided at PHASE_SHIFT updates Config.General.UserGuided before the phase transition fires

items.name: Auto-commit: staging strategies and commit execution
items.description: Implement AutoCommit function with all five commit strategies (strict, all-specs, scoped, tracked, all). Implement hash registration on completed specs after specifying COMPLETE. Wire into advance at COMPLETE, planning ACCEPT, and implementing COMMIT when enable_commits=true.
steps: In state/git.go: implement AutoCommit(projectRoot, strategy, stageTargets, message) (string, error)
strict strategy: git add <each path in stageTargets>
all-specs strategy: git add <each spec directory in stageTargets>
scoped strategy: git add <each domain dir in stageTargets>
tracked strategy: git add -u
all strategy: git add -A
After git add: run git -C projectRoot commit -m message, capture output
Run git -C projectRoot rev-parse HEAD to get full commit hash
In advanceSpecifying COMPLETE (when EnableCommits=true): call AutoCommit with all-specs strategy, stageTargets = completed spec file paths; then register hash on all CompletedSpecs
In advancePlanning ACCEPT (when EnableCommits=true): call AutoCommit with strict strategy, stageTargets = CurrentPlan.File + referenced notes
In advanceImplementing COMMIT (when EnableCommits=true): call AutoCommit with scoped strategy, stageTargets = CurrentPlanDomain dir

tests.description: AutoCommit with 'all' strategy runs 'git add -A' then 'git commit -m <message>' and returns a non-empty hash
AutoCommit with 'tracked' strategy runs 'git add -u'
AutoCommit with 'strict' strategy runs 'git add' with specific file paths
AutoCommit returns error when git commit fails (non-zero exit), state does not advance
specifying COMPLETE with EnableCommits=true commits and registers hash on all CompletedSpecs.CommitHashes
AutoCommit when no files are staged (nothing to commit) skips commit execution and prints notice; state still advances
planning ACCEPT with EnableCommits=true and no staged files skips commit and prints notice

items.name: Remove add-commit and reconcile-commit commands
items.description: Delete cmd/addcommit.go and cmd/reconcilecommit.go. Remove AddCommitToSpec, ReconcileCommit, GitShowFiles from git.go. Remove command registrations from root command.
steps: Delete forgectl/cmd/addcommit.go
Delete forgectl/cmd/reconcilecommit.go
In cmd/root.go: remove AddCommand calls for add-commit and reconcile-commit
In state/git.go: remove AddCommitToSpec, ReconcileCommit, GitShowFiles functions
Keep GitHashExists and GitRepoRoot (may be used elsewhere)
Update tests: remove any add-commit and reconcile-commit test cases from cmd/commands_test.go and state/advance_test.go

tests.description: Running 'forgectl add-commit' prints 'unknown command' error
Running 'forgectl reconcile-commit' prints 'unknown command' error

items.name: Output format updates for new states and behaviors
items.description: Update PrintAdvanceOutput and PrintStatus for: generate_planning_queue states (ORIENT/REFINE/PHASE_SHIFT), SELF_REVIEW, Specs/Refs plural in IMPLEMENT, DONE implementing variants, COMMIT enable_commits variants, ORIENT Next: info, STUDY_CODE specs list, updated REFINE messages, COMPLETE enable_commits variants, ACCEPT enable_commits variants.
steps: Add printGeneratePlanningQueueOutput(w, s) for ORIENT, REFINE states
Update printPhaseShiftOutput to handle From=generate_planning_queue, From=planning/To=planning, From=implementing/To=planning, From=implementing/To=implementing
Update printImplementOrient to show 'Specs:' (plural), 'Refs:' (plural) for each item
Update printImplementOrient (ORIENT after COMMIT) to show 'Next: N unblocked items' or 'Next: L1 Core — N items: [...]' or '(final layer)' message
Update printImplementDone to show domain + 'Advance to continue' when plans remain; show session summary when no plans remain
Update printImplementCommit to vary action by enable_commits
Update printPlanningAccept to vary action by enable_commits
Update printSpecifyingComplete to vary action by enable_commits
Update printStudyCode to include spec files list
Update printSelfReview output
Update printRefine (specifying and planning) to vary action by enable_eval_output
Update printEvaluate (all phases) to vary --eval-report guidance by enable_eval_output

tests.description: generate_planning_queue ORIENT output contains 'Generated:' line with plan-queue.json path
generate_planning_queue REFINE output instructs architect to review plan-queue.json
IMPLEMENT output shows 'Specs:' with multiple lines when item has multiple spec refs
DONE (implementing) with plans remaining shows domain and 'Advance to continue to next domain'
DONE (implementing) session complete shows aggregate summary across all domains
COMMIT output with enable_commits=true says 'Advance with --message'
COMMIT output with enable_commits=false says 'Advance to continue'
ORIENT (implementing, layer complete) output shows 'Next: L1 Core — N items: [...]'

items.name: Eval command output: enable_eval_output gating and new states
items.description: Update PrintEvalOutput to gate the REPORT OUTPUT section on enable_eval_output. Add eval output for specifying RECONCILE_EVAL (loads reconcile-eval.md) and CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL (loads cross-reference-eval.md). Embed evaluator files in binary using go:embed.
steps: Add //go:embed directives for all five evaluator .md files (spec-eval, plan-eval, impl-eval, reconcile-eval, cross-reference-eval)
Update planning eval output: include REPORT OUTPUT section only when enable_eval_output=true
Update implementing eval output: same enable_eval_output gating
Add PrintReconcileEvalOutput(w, s): domain list, reconcile-eval.md contents, staged diff instruction, conditional REPORT OUTPUT section
Add PrintCrossRefEvalOutput(w, s): domain+spec list, cross-reference-eval.md contents, conditional REPORT OUTPUT section
In cmd/eval.go: allow eval in RECONCILE_EVAL and CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL states; dispatch to correct Print function

tests.description: Planning eval output with enable_eval_output=true includes '--- REPORT OUTPUT ---' section
Planning eval output with enable_eval_output=false omits '--- REPORT OUTPUT ---' section
eval command in RECONCILE_EVAL state outputs reconcile-eval.md contents
eval command in CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL state outputs cross-reference-eval.md contents
eval command outside of evaluation states returns error naming current state

items.name: status --verbose flag and verbose output
items.description: Add --verbose / -v flag to the status command. When set, append full session overview after the standard status output: queue contents, completed items with eval history, per-item implementing detail.
steps: In cmd/status.go: add --verbose / -v BoolVarP flag
Pass verbose bool to state.PrintStatus
In state/output.go PrintStatus: when verbose=true, append queue section, completed section, prior phase summaries
Specifying verbose: list remaining queue entries (name, domain), list completed specs (name, domain, rounds, eval history, commit hashes)
Planning verbose: list remaining plan queue entries, list completed plans
Implementing verbose: list remaining items by layer with passes/rounds per item

tests.description: status without --verbose outputs current state and action only (no queue/completed sections)
status --verbose in specifying phase shows completed specs with eval history
status -v in implementing phase shows per-item passes/rounds detail

items.name: validate CLI command
items.description: Implement 'forgectl validate <file>' command. Auto-detects file type from top-level JSON keys. Supports --type override. Uses existing validation functions. Does not require a state file.
steps: Create forgectl/cmd/validate.go
Register validateCmd with root command in cmd/root.go
Accept positional argument: path to JSON file
Add --type flag with values: spec-queue, plan-queue, plan
Auto-detect logic: unmarshal into map[string]interface{}, check top-level keys: 'specs' → spec-queue; 'plans' → plan-queue; 'context'+'items'+'layers' → plan
Call appropriate validation function
For plan validation: pass filepath.Dir(file) as baseDir for refs path resolution
Print '✓ <filename>: valid <type>' on success (exit 0)
Print 'FAIL: N errors in <filename>\n  <err1>...' on failure (exit 1)

tests.description: validate spec-queue.json auto-detects type and prints valid message
validate plan-queue.json auto-detects type and prints valid message
validate plan.json auto-detects type, resolves refs relative to plan dir, prints valid message
validate with invalid plan.json prints FAIL with error list and exits 1
validate with --type override that conflicts with file content (e.g. --type spec-queue on a plan.json) fails validation
validate non-existent file prints file-not-found error and exits 1
validate with unrecognized top-level keys (auto-detection fails) prints error 'cannot determine file type' and exits 1
validate with invalid JSON (parse error) prints parse error with details and exits 1
validate with --type plan on a valid plan.json succeeds (type override bypasses auto-detection)
validate runs successfully without a forgectl-state.json present (no session required)
validate with empty object {} fails auto-detection and prints cannot-determine-type error

items.name: eval command extension for RECONCILE_EVAL and CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL
items.description: Extend the eval command to be valid in specifying RECONCILE_EVAL and CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL states, in addition to existing planning and implementing EVALUATE states.
steps: In cmd/eval.go: update valid-state check to include RECONCILE_EVAL and CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL
Dispatch to PrintReconcileEvalOutput when phase=specifying and state=RECONCILE_EVAL
Dispatch to PrintCrossRefEvalOutput when phase=specifying and state=CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL
Error message when called outside valid states: 'eval is only valid in EVALUATE, RECONCILE_EVAL, or CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL (current: <state>)'

tests.description: eval in specifying RECONCILE_EVAL outputs reconciliation context
eval in specifying CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL outputs cross-reference context
eval in specifying DRAFT returns error naming current state

items.name: Activity logging: JSONL session logs and pruning
items.description: Implement JSONL activity logging to ~/.forgectl/logs/. Each session has one log file named <phase>-<session_id_prefix>.jsonl. Log init and advance commands. Prune old files at init based on retention_days and max_files config. Logging is best-effort (never blocks primary workflow).
steps: Create forgectl/state/logging.go
Define LogEntry struct: ts (ISO 8601), cmd, phase, prev_state (advance only), state, detail (map[string]interface{})
Implement logDir() string — returns ~/.forgectl/logs/ (expand ~)
Implement logFileName(phase, sessionID) string — '<phase>-<first8chars>.jsonl'
Implement WriteLogEntry(sessionID, startPhase string, entry LogEntry) error — opens/creates file in append mode, marshals entry, writes line
Implement PruneLogFiles(logDir string, retentionDays int, maxFiles int) — delete files older than retention_days, then trim to maxFiles newest
In cmd/init.go: after state file created, call PruneLogFiles then write init log entry (best-effort, ignore errors)
In cmd/advance.go: after state transition and state.Save(), write advance log entry with prev_state captured before advance (best-effort)

Build detail map per state/command: init: {batch, guided}; evaluate: {round, verdict, eval_report (if EnableEvalOutput)}; implementing commit: {layer, batch, items}; implementing orient: {layer, unblocked, remaining}; generate_planning_queue advances: {state transition context}
When logs.enabled=false in Config: skip all logging

tests.description: init writes a JSONL log entry with cmd='init' and phase/state fields
advance writes a JSONL log entry with cmd='advance', prev_state, and new state
evaluate advance with EnableEvalOutput=true includes eval_report in log detail
evaluate advance with EnableEvalOutput=false omits eval_report from log detail
PruneLogFiles deletes files older than retention_days
PruneLogFiles trims to max_files when more files exist (keeps newest)
Session spanning multiple phases uses single log file named after the initial phase
Logging failure (unwritable directory) does not prevent advance command from succeeding
logs.enabled=false skips all log file creation and writes
PruneLogFiles with retention_days=0 and max_files=0 performs no deletion
PruneLogFiles with both retention_days and max_files set applies age-based deletion first, then count-based on remaining files
add-queue-item command does not produce a log entry (only init and advance are logged)
status command does not produce a log entry (read-only commands skip logging)
